west and north-west. The remaining deities in the outer compart-
ments, 20 in number, own two squares each. The remaining 4
deities headed by Aryaman preside over three squares each (begin-
ning with the east of Brahman).
[In diagram 1 there are altogether 81 squares or compartments
owned by 45 deities : In the four outer corners there are 5x4=20
deities having 20 squares i.e. one square each. Then there arc
20 deities (5X4) in the four outer quarters, each owning two com-
partments, which comes to 40 compartments. Then there are four
deities in the four inner quarters having three divisions each i.e. 12
divisions. Lastly, Brahman who is in the centre of the site, owns or
occupies 9 squares.
(i) Deities ruling over single compartments :
Sikhin, Parjanya, Diti, Apah and Apavatsa; Antariksa, Anila,
Pusan, Savitra; and Savita, Mfga, Pil|r, Dauvarika, Jaya and Indra;
and Papayaksman, Roga, Ahi (serpent), Rudra and Rajayaksman.
(ii) Those ruling over two squares :
